name: TUMORAL CALCINOSIS
definition: LIPOCALCINOGRANULOMATOSIS = TEUTSCHLäNDER DISEASE rare familial metabolic disorder characterized by solitary or multiple painless, periarticular disease with progressive large nodular juxtaarticular calcified soft-tissue masses in patients

demographics.Etiology: autosomal dominant (¹/³) with variable clinical expressivity; unknown biochemical
demographics.Path: multilocular cystic lesions with creamy white fluid (calcium hydroxyapatite crystals
demographics.Age: onset mostly within 1st / 2nd decade (range of 1-79 years); M÷F = 1÷1; predominantly in

discriminating_features: cystic appearance with radiolucent septa (= connective tissue)
"sedimentation" sign = fluid-fluid levels with milk-of-calcium consistency
inhomogeneous high SI on T2WI (in spite of large amount of calcium) of 2 distinctive

XRAY: amorphous cystic multilobulated homogeneously calcified soft-tissue mass of 1-20 cm
underlying bones NORMAL without erosion / osseous destruction
CT: cystic appearance with radiolucent septa (= connective tissue)
"sedimentation" sign = fluid-fluid levels with milk-of-calcium consistency
NUC: ↑ tracer uptake of soft-tissue mass on bone scan
MR: inhomogeneous high SI on T2WI (in spite of large amount of calcium) of 2 distinctive
bright nodular pattern with alternating areas of high signal intensity and signal void
inhomogeneous lesion with low SI on T1WI
Bone: patchy areas of calcification in medullary cavity (= calcific myelitis)

clinical.Prognosis: tendency for recurrence after incomplete excision
